topics: Materials and their Properties - Elastic and Plastic Behaviour of Metals
Design for Static and Fatigue Loads
Suggestions - Problem Project: Students can explore innovative approaches to design processes, integrating preferred numbers, standards, and codes into practical design solutions
Rolling Contact Bearing - Design of Bearings
Stresses and Deflection of Helical Springs

Suggestion - Problem Project: Students will have the autonomy to explore and design bearing and spring systems based on specified performance criteria and real-world applications. The project will integrate concepts from rolling contact bearings, sliding contact bearings, and springs, focusing on design considerations, material selection, and performance analysis
Virtual or Formative Number of Teeth
Heat Dissipation, Lubrication of Gears, Merits and Demerits of Each Type of Gears
Design of Spur Gear and Helical Gear

Suggestion - Discipline Project: Students will explore the design and analysis of gear systems, focusing on both spur gears and helical gears. The project will involve studying gear classification, nomenclature, tooth profiles, materials selection, and gear failure modes. Students will apply theoretical concepts such as the Law of Gearing, virtual number of teeth, and gear tooth strength calculations
Design of Internal Combustion Engine Parts - Piston

Suggestion - Discipline Project: Students will explore various aspects of clutch systems, integrating knowledge from clutches, brakes, and the design of internal combustion engine parts. The project will involve both theoretical exploration and practical application, aligning with the discipline Project model where students have the freedom to choose specific aspects to explore within the defined theme
